tweets.text: Acting Deputy Secretary John Bass swore in 100 new Civil Service colleagues in @FSIatState's 199th Civil Service Orientation class. Looking forward to their efforts to make the Department stronger, more effective, and more diverse. https://t.co/Lu9wchmJX2

Advancing the economic status of women and girls reduces poverty and promotes sustainable economic growth, increases access to education, improves health outcomes, advances political stability, and fosters democracy. Learn about recent initiatives: https://t.co/MRDEY9cMCT https://t.co/nrDa5qMARQ

As #WomensHistoryMonth comes to a close, we celebrate the trailblazing team of U.S. Government women leaders charged with tackling Illegal, unreported, and unregulated fishing. Read to learn more from @scidiplomacyusa: https://t.co/ZBrAmLrO05

The ability of women and girls to participate safely, freely, and equally in political life and in society is a defining feature of democracy. Learn about the U.S.’s ongoing work to advance women’s political and civic participation: https://t.co/dRiq33iBh8 https://t.co/FP2695OGHy

Alba Rueda is Argentina's current Special Envoy for Sexual Orientation and Gender Identity. Rueda is a driving force behind transgender rights. Her hope is to establish an LGBTQI+ foreign policy agenda and mainstream it into the various negotiations. #IWOC https://t.co/indmlZaKVa
Today, we celebrate the visibility of trans and non-binary people in society and advocate for their human rights. Trans rights = human rights. #TDOV https://t.co/A9hzhzWwaK

The U.S. and international partners continue to support survivors of the devastating earthquakes in Türkiye and Syria, which killed more than 50,000 people and displaced at least 3 million. Total U.S. support for the response is now more than $235 million. https://t.co/qQ12X1g862

After the horrific earthquakes in Türkiye and Syria, Gizem White, a Turkish-American entrepreneur, asked herself “What can I do from here?” It turns out she could do a lot from D.C. https://t.co/r67OVrtW0i

.@SecBlinken: Women and girls are leading the charge for their rights — and for human rights and democracy around the world. The U.S. stands in solidarity with these women and all who are working for women’s full, free, and equal participation around the world. https://t.co/4ayIJnk2oI

In the latest DipNote, Retired Ambassador Ruth Davis shares the achievements of 14 outstanding African American women in U.S. Foreign Policy. Each of these women has a compelling story accompanying their rise through the ranks. https://t.co/Mz8LKIn92r

https://t.co/2g86NzQ2Sz

In another successful shipment, the U.S. donated 270,000+ COVID-19 vaccine doses to Honduras in partnership with #COVAX. With over 7 million vaccine doses to the people of 🇭🇳, we are proud to help protect communities across the country from the harmful impacts of the pandemic.
